As their friendship deepens, the queen begins to see a changing world through new eyes, joyfully reclaiming her humanity.

programme.title: The Tournament

programme.title: Chess Masters: The Endgame

programme.title: Clive Myrie's Italian Roadtrip
programme.desc: Starting at his favourite holiday spot on the Amalfi coast, Clive goes inland to a mozzarella factory, where he discovers how to make everyone's favourite stringy cheese. Next, he heads to Capri, where he makes his wife Catherine a pair of the Capri sandals made famous by Jackie Kennedy and spends the evening singing Italian folk songs on stage at a club favoured by A-listers like Beyonce and Jay Z.

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The dry season is now upon the animals of the Serengeti, the toughest of times for some but a food bonanza for others. The increasing drought turns the waterhole into a death trap for the herds, but for the lions it's a gift and Kali and her cubs are finally able to enjoy life back in the heart of the pride.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and a team of experts examine artefacts offered up by the public at Knebworth House. Items featured include a marine chronometer and a diamond brooch.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team are in Aberystwyth at the Old College, the original site of the first university in Wales. The experts on duty are David Harper and Christina Trevanion. Christina values a Victorian Claret jug and David gets all of a flutter over a large selection of antique fans. Meanwhile, Paul Martin visits Laugharne, the home of his literary hero, Dylan Thomas.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Amy Gledhill, Jacqui Joseph, Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen and Tim Vine test their general knowledge skills on this week's Richard Osman's House of Games: Champions.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: The tour reaches Bamburgh as Vernon Kay and unreliable guide Paul Gorton ask comedian Susan Calman, internet star Max Fosh and DJ Noreen Khan to sort the accurate from the adventurous in Bamburgh.

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: This week, the accounts of some of the women brutally repressed by the Iranian government during the 2022 Women, Life, Freedom protests and Katy Watson examines the Australian social media ban.

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: From a mythic past, when kelpies lurked in the lochs of the Trossachs, Paul travels through old Perthshire. He lends a hand bringing in the cattle and hears how Rob Roy MacGregor might not have been the romantic figure of popular fiction. Was he a hero or just a cattle thief? At Rob Roy's graveside, Paul meets a man who casts doubts on the clansman's last resting place.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie asks the questions in another semi-final. Specialist subjects include Clint Eastwood's western films and the footballer Cyrille Regis.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The quarter-final stage nears its conclusion. Two places in the semi-finals are still up for grabs - both teams are hoping to take one of them. Amol Rajan asks the questions.

programme.title: Clash of the Super Powers: America v China
programme.desc: Donald Trump comes to office in 2017 and upends America's relationship with Beijing. He promises to 'make China pay', but when he starts a trade war, Xi Jinping fights back.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob are fishing for tench, a fish they have not targeted since the first series. They arrive at Rocklands Mere, a secluded hidden gem in the quaint village of Rocklands St Peter, and settle down to fish.

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: All around is a blackened smoking wasteland, the aftermath of the huge fire. Torrential rain brings hope and the land is reborn into a green paradise as water quenches the scorched earth. Mirroring the changes in the landscape, the cycle of death and rebirth comes to the fore as the Serengeti's elders prepare their young for adulthood and a life on their own.

This edition comes from Knebworth House in Hertfordshire and features a pair of revealing female figures originally displayed in a French brothel, an unusual table clock with a floating turtle which tells the time, a chemist's mortar dating from 1573 and a Victorian toilet given as a present.

Paul Martin gets excited by iconic pop autographs and heads off to the nearby town of Dudley to find out about saddlemaking there from the First World War onwards.

Based on Amanda Foreman's bestselling novel `Georgiana, Duchess of Devonshire'.

programme.desc: Newly-weds Shariqa and Imani have struggled to keep their renovation project on schedule or on budget in Cambridgeshire. With just £10,000 to add a kitchen and transform the ground floor, Stacey has her work cut out.

programme.desc: When Doug and Carrie offer to pay damages for rear-ending Deacon and Kelly's car, it causes friction between the two couples. What was the cause of the accident?

programme.title: The King of Queens

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra get locked in a three-week long `unspoken' battle over who's going to put the suitcase away.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Peter attempts to bury the hatchet between himself, Robert and Ray, Robert suspects he's up to no good and assigns Ray to keep an eye on him.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The radio station goes from bad to worse without Roz and Frasier, and management is compelled to change the programming. Our two friends are certain that they will get their old jobs back.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A friendly woman comes to Frasier's rescue when he is out Christmas shopping. She recognises him and he offers to return the favour, but the last thing he is expecting is to end up on a blind date with her daughter.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frasier is arrested after unwittingly giving a ride to a prostitute. To make matters worse, he must explain the public scandal to his visiting 11-year-old son Frederick.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Marge is bitten by their dog, Santa's Little Helper, the family sets out to discover the dog's tragic past. With the help of a psychologist, they try to help him get rid of his traumas.

programme.desc: When City Councilman Kevin Fogarty comes to Cheers and woos the crowd with his politics, Frasier places a bet that Woody would get at least 10 percent of the vote if he ran for city councillor.

programme.desc: Robert and Amy exchange vows in a wedding ceremony that has the Barone family tradition written all over it.

programme.title: In Our Blood: The Forever Chemicals Scandal
programme.desc: In almost every respect, Bentham is a quintessential Yorkshire town: proud, close-knit and set against breathtaking countryside. Two years ago, an environmental journalist's investigation revealed it to be the most contaminated place ever recorded in the UK for PFAS - toxic so-called forever chemicals, some of which are linked to cancers and other serious illnesses. For decades, the town's main employer, Angus Fire, produced firefighting foam now believed to be the likely source of the pollution. The company routinely manufactured and tested this foam on site; the government ultimately banned it in 2025. This groundbreaking documentary reveals how those chemicals may have spread beyond the factory boundary and how a small town is coming to terms with what it means for them.

programme.title: WhatsApp Obsession: The Murder of Stephanie Hansen
programme.desc: On the 30th of December, 2022, 39-year-old Stephanie Hansen was brutally stabbed to death in her London home - her housemate and friend Sheldon Rodrigues initially appeared grief-stricken, yet behind his facade lay a chilling obsession. In the months before Stephanie's death, Rodrigues planted secret cameras and recording devices in their home and bombarded her with coercive WhatsApp messages - when Steph began a new relationship, his jealousy turned deadly. Through exclusive access to police bodycam footage, WhatsApp records and court evidence, this film reveals how the Metropolitan Police exposed Rodrigues' lies and uncovered the disturbing level of surveillance Steph was subjected to - featuring interviews with Steph's family, friends and the detectives who investigated the case, this powerful documentary unravels a story of obsession, control, and the ultimate betrayal.

programme.title: Be Cool, Scooby-Doo!

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter swaps lives with Supreme Court Justice Brett Kavanaugh and is forced to make a ruling on the constitutionality of gay marriage, meanwhile, Brett tries to become friends with Quagmire, Cleveland and Joe.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter and Quagmire accidentally switch underwear, they begin to mimic the other's behavior; Stewie and Doug agree to commit each other's murders.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Continuing a popular show tradition, Brian and Stewie respond to viewer mail. Then, Peter encourages Joe to have a one-night stand with his attractive new partner in order to even the score with Bonnie for her own indiscretions.

programme.title: American Dad!

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Lewis and Hathaway enter the world of St Gerard's Hall when a visiting female bishop is found dead after drinking poisoned wine. Was she was murdered because her liberal views contradicted those of the reactionary religious leaders of the college - or is something more sinister afoot? When another two deaths occur, both mirroring murders from Jacobean revenge tragedy, Lewis and Hathaway realise that something twisted is afoot.

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Detective Constable Endeavour Morse returns to Oxford to investigate the disappearance of a missing schoolgirl.

programme.title: Doc Martin

programme.title: Rosemary and Thyme
programme.desc: The Ligurian coast of Italy is no holiday for Rosemary and Laura as they create a restaurant garden for Emma Standish. During a tour of the glorious Giardini Tremonti, the girls are propelled into a mystery when a woman in their party is killed. As they investigate her identity, there are further revelations - and further deaths.

programme.title: Rosemary and Thyme
programme.desc: Rosemary and Laura become involved when an archaeologist, who posed as Rosemary to gain access to the formerly perfect gardens of a large estate, is found murdered.

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot is called in 14 years after the fact to re-examine the murder of renowned painter Amyas Crale - whose wife Caroline was hanged for the crime - when their grown-up daughter Lucy presents him with a letter written to her by her mother, declaring her innocence.

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: In the sleepy English village of St Mary Mead, one man - Colonel Protheroe - is intensely disliked by everyone. Even the vicar has good reason to want to murder him. Then Protheroe is found with a bullet through the head, slumped over the desk in the vicarage study. The obvious suspects seem to be the colonel's wife or her lover - but have they been set up? Miss Marple unpicks the truth behind the crime.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott delve into the murder of a visitor to Plummer's Relish factory, and discover that the dead man was employed by a rival company. Further investigation reveals a web of secrets and lies within the Plummer family, and a campaign to frighten the family matriarch - but why?

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: DCI Vera Stanhope must unravel the circumstances of self-styled entrepreneur Freddie Gill's death when his body is found by bailiffs, attempting to repossess his house. Freddie is estranged from his wife and son, who have returned to living with her formidable mother Tina, her volatile brother Lee and her taciturn uncle Darren at the family's beloved pub. Freddie's legacy of unpaid debts and ruined lives leaves no shortage of suspects.

programme.title: After the Flood
programme.desc: When Jo discovers that the two dead men and an accomplice targeted a nearby chemical company belonging to Alan Benson, she must find this third person before it's too late.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
